rulings.Case Title: JIM D PARRISH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted with respect to all causes of action alleged against Hennessy. The second and third causes of action do not specify any product for which defendant is responsible. (Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 80). The fourteenth and fifteenth causes of action allege that the brake shoe grinding machines ¿while being used in a manner that was reasonably foreseeable, failed to protect plaintiff from exposure to asbestos fibers released from asbestos-containing brake lining caused by use of said brake shoe grinding machines and related products and equipment, resulting in severe and permanent injury to plaintiff.¿ (Complaint ¶¶ 151, 156). The machines are alleged to be ¿defective and unsafe for their intended purpose in that said products caused, and failed to prevent, the inhalation and ingestion of asbestos fibers by decedent. (Complaint ¶¶ 152, 157). A product manufacturer has no duty to persons whose injury results from defects in the products of others. (Garman v. Magic Chef, Inc. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 634; Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co., Inc. 171 (2009) Cal.App.4th 564). The complaint does not allege that defendant sold an asbestos-containing product or that it had any control over the design and manufacture of the asbestos-containing brake linings used in conjunction with its machine. Tellez-Cordova v. Campbell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co. (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable because the power tools at issue in that case had no function until they were used with toxic-containing disks. (Id. at 584 (¿[U]se with the specified wheels, discs, and grinders was the inevitable use.¿). Here, there is no allegation that defendant¿s machines could only function in conjunction with asbestos-containing brake linings, or that the machines and linings formed a sin

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HENRY SCHECHINGER VS. AMERICAN STANDARD, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Autozone West, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiff¿s complaint is denied. The complaint pleads sufficient facts supporting the prayer for punitive damages. Defendant did not cite, nor is the court aware of any, authority requiring that a plaintiff allege facts supporting a request for punitive damages that are specific to each of the defendants sued. A request for punitive damages may be based on a non-fraud products liability claim provided that sufficient facts showing malice, fraud and/or oppression are alleged. Defendant. must answer the complaint within 10 days of the hearing date. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recover Unreimbursed Costs And Fees Against Vasquez, Estrada & Conway And Their Clients
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Designated defense counsel Berry & Berry¿s motion to recover unreimbursed costs and fees against Vasquez, Estrada & Conway and their clients is granted. No opposition filed. =(220/HEK)
